title: Phase 3 — Docs and rollout for macOS device owner authentication
summary: Document the macOS-only device owner authentication model, update user-facing command guidance, and define rollout/migration steps for authenticated secret reveal.
inputs: Current CLI behavior for `secret get` and planned `secret reveal` flow
macOS authentication model using LocalAuthentication with `.deviceOwnerAuthentication`
Existing README and security-related docs under docs/
outputs: Updated user documentation for authenticated secret reveal
Rollout notes covering migration from plaintext flags to `secret reveal`
Troubleshooting guidance for Touch ID / Apple Watch / password fallback behavior
Manual verification checklist for macOS authentication UX
acceptance_criteria: Documentation clearly states that plaintext secret reveal on macOS requires device owner authentication.
Documentation distinguishes `secret get` from `secret reveal` and explains intended usage.
Docs state that Touch ID and Apple Watch behavior depends on macOS capabilities and user settings, not app-specific guarantees.
Migration guidance covers deprecation or compatibility behavior for `--plain`, `--show`, and `--force-plain`.
Troubleshooting guidance covers missing helper, unavailable authentication, cancelled authentication, and Keychain access failures.
Rollout notes explain the fail-closed behavior for unsupported platforms or missing authentication support.
risks: User confusion if `secret get` and `secret reveal` semantics are not documented precisely.
Over-promising Apple Watch-specific behavior when actual factor selection is controlled by macOS.
Documentation drift if implementation details change during backend integration.
notes: Prefer wording that promises OS-mediated device owner authentication rather than a specific factor.
Keep security messaging explicit: discoverability of commands does not bypass OS authentication.
implementation_steps: Update README and relevant docs to describe authenticated reveal flow.
Add a command behavior section for `secret get` vs `secret reveal`.
Write migration notes for legacy plaintext flags.
Add troubleshooting and manual verification checklist.
Add rollout notes describing platform limits and fail-closed behavior.

checks: Docs reference the correct command names and expected behavior.
Acceptance criteria from the plan are reflected in user-facing documentation.
Security language is consistent with `.deviceOwnerAuthentication` semantics.
manual_steps: Review docs against planned CLI contract.
Confirm no documentation claims app-level control over Apple Watch selection.
Confirm troubleshooting covers authentication cancel and unavailable cases.
